message:
fix(web): harden session middleware against fixation attacks and entropy failures

Session middleware rewritten to prevent session-fixation attacks more robustly:
- Unknown-but-well-formed session IDs no longer automatically allocate a
  server-side session; the handler must mutate session state for persistence
- Session ID generation now returns Result, propagating getrandom errors as
  500 responses instead of panicking on entropy failure
- generate_session_id_with() extracted to accept caller-supplied entropy for
  testability

SessionData gains mark_clean() to reset the per-request dirty bit after
load and save, preventing spurious re-persistence of unchanged sessions.

MemoryStore::load() and save() now call mark_clean() so loaded data starts
with modified=false and saved data does not carry stale dirty state.

Error responses use a constant INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR_BODY to avoid leaking
internal details, consistent with the earlier request_region security fix.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
